feat(cli): Implement mark command for feedback recording

Implements the mark command - a key user-facing tool for recording
helpful/harmful feedback on playbook bullets:

## Usage

  cm mark <bullet-id> --helpful [--reason "why"] [--session path]
  cm mark <bullet-id> --harmful [--reason "why"] [--session path]

## Implementation Details

### Bullet Location Strategy

1. Load merged playbook (global + repo) for initial lookup
2. If bullet found, determine source file for saving:
   - Check global playbook first (config.playbookPath)
   - Fall back to repo playbook (.cass/playbook.yaml)
3. Reload the specific source file for atomic update

This ensures feedback is saved to the correct file in cascaded
playbook scenarios.

### Feedback Recording

- Creates FeedbackEvent with timestamp, type, sessionId, reason
- Appends to bullet.feedbackEvents array
- Increments helpfulCount or harmfulCount counters
- Triggers maturity state machine evaluation

### State Machine Integration

After recording feedback:
- Calls calculateMaturityState() to evaluate new maturity
- Calls getEffectiveScore() to compute updated score
- If harmful and score drops below threshold: warns about deprecation

### Output

- Human-readable summary with updated counts and score
- --json flag for machine-readable output with full bullet state

### Validation

- Requires exactly one of --helpful or --harmful
- Validates bullet exists before modification
- Reports clear error if bullet not found in any playbook

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.3","title":"Binary Compilation + Distribution","description":"# Feature: Binary Compilation + Distribution

## Purpose
Compile cass-memory to standalone binaries for easy distribution.
Users shouldn't need Bun installed to use the tool.

## Build Targets
- Linux x64: dist/cass-memory-linux-x64
- macOS ARM64: dist/cass-memory-darwin-arm64
- macOS x64: dist/cass-memory-darwin-x64
- Windows x64: dist/cass-memory-windows-x64.exe

## Build Commands
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --outfile dist/cass-memory",
    "build:all": "npm run build:linux && npm run build:macos-arm && npm run build:macos-x64 && npm run build:windows",
    "build:linux": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-linux-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-linux-x64",
    "build:macos-arm": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-darwin-arm64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-darwin-arm64",
    "build:macos-x64": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-darwin-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-darwin-x64",
    "build:windows": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-windows-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-windows-x64.exe"
  }
}
```

## Binary Size Considerations
- Base Bun runtime: ~50MB
- Application code: ~200KB
- Dependencies: ~5MB (mostly AI SDK)
- Total: ~55MB per binary

## Distribution Options
1. GitHub Releases (primary)
2. Homebrew formula (macOS)
3. npm/bun global install (for developers)

## Installation Instructions
```bash
# macOS (Homebrew)
brew install cass-memory

# Direct download
curl -L https://github.com/org/cass-memory/releases/latest/download/cass-memory-darwin-arm64 -o cass-memory
chmod +x cass-memory
sudo mv cass-memory /usr/local/bin/

# npm/bun
bun install -g cass-memory
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:09.629265-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:09.629265-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.3","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:09.630419-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.3","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:26.253012-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.4","title":"Documentation: README.md + Examples","description":"# Feature: Documentation

## Purpose
Comprehensive documentation for users and contributors.

## Files to Create

### README.md
```markdown
# cass-memory

Agent-agnostic reflection and memory system for coding agents.
Implements the ACE (Agentic Context Engineering) framework.

## Quick Start

\`\`\`bash
# Install
brew install cass-memory  # or download binary

# Initialize
cass-memory init

# Get context before a task
cass-memory context "Fix authentication timeout" --json

# Mark rules as helpful/harmful
cass-memory mark b-abc123 --helpful
cass-memory mark b-def456 --harmful --reason "Caused test failures"

# Run reflection on recent sessions
cass-memory reflect --days 7

# Check playbook health
cass-memory stats
cass-memory doctor
\`\`\`

## How It Works

cass-memory implements three memory layers:

1. **Episodic Memory** (cass): Raw session logs from all agents
2. **Working Memory** (Diary): Structured session summaries
3. **Procedural Memory** (Playbook): Distilled rules with feedback tracking

## Configuration

\`\`\`bash
# Set your LLM provider
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
# or
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

# Configure
cass-memory config --provider anthropic --model claude-sonnet-4-20250514
\`\`\`

## Integration

Add to your AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md:
\`\`\`
cass-memory project --format agents.md >> AGENTS.md
\`\`\`

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| init | Initialize configuration |
| context | Get pre-task context |
| diary | Generate diary from session |
| reflect | Run reflection pipeline |
| mark | Record feedback |
| forget | Permanently block pattern |
| stats | Show playbook health |
| doctor | System health check |
| audit | Check for violations |
| project | Export to AGENTS.md |
\`\`\`

### Examples Directory
- examples/basic-workflow.md
- examples/team-setup.md
- examples/ci-integration.md

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-3pb","title":"[Playbook] updateBulletCounter - Increment helpful/harmful count","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function updateBulletCounter(
  playbook: Playbook,
  bulletId: string,
  type: "helpful" | "harmful"
): void
```

## Purpose
**DEPRECATED**: Legacy function to increment helpful/harmful counters without timestamp tracking.

## Deprecation Notice
This function is maintained for backward compatibility only. **New code should use recordFeedbackEvent()** instead.

## Why Deprecated?
- No timestamp tracking → cannot calculate confidence decay
- No event history → cannot analyze feedback patterns
- No session attribution → cannot trace feedback source
- Prevents effective confidence scoring

## Implementation
```typescript
const bullet = findBullet(playbook, bulletId);
if (!bullet) return;

if (type === "helpful") {
  bullet.helpfulCount++;
} else {
  bullet.harmfulCount++;
}
bullet.updatedAt = now();
```

## Migration Path
Replace:
```typescript
updateBulletCounter(playbook, bulletId, "helpful");
```

With:
```typescript
recordFeedbackEvent(playbook, bulletId, "helpful", {
  timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  sessionId: currentSessionId,
  context: "user marked as helpful"
});
```

## Removal Timeline
- **Current**: Maintained for compatibility
- **Future**: Will be removed in schema v3
- **Action**: Migrate all callers to recordFeedbackEvent()

## Dependencies
- findBullet()

- recordFeedbackEvent() - modern replacement
- calculateEffectiveScore() - requires events, not just counts","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:56.393319-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:25.058609-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-3yb","title":"[Cass] cassExpand - Get context around a line","description":"Implement cassExpand() to get context around a specific line.

## Signature
```typescript
async function cassExpand(
  sessionPath: string, 
  lineNumber: number, 
  contextLines = 3,
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<string | null>
```

## CLI Command
`cass expand "{sessionPath}" -n {lineNumber} -C {contextLines} --json`

## Implementation
- Use execFile() to run expand command
- Return expanded context as string
- Return null on error

## Error Handling
- Return null if session not found
- Return null if line number out of range
- No retries (expand is deterministic)

## Parameters
- sessionPath: Full path to session file
- lineNumber: Target line (1-indexed)
- contextLines: Lines before/after target (default: 3)

## Output
Returns context window around target line:
```
35: Previous line 3
36: Previous line 2  
37: Previous line 1
38: >>> TARGET LINE <<<
39: Next line 1
40: Next line 2
41: Next line 3
```

## Usage
Used to get surrounding context for search hits:
```typescript
const hits = await cassSearch("bug fix");
for (const hit of hits) {
  const context = await cassExpand(hit.sessionPath, hit.lineNumber, 5);
  console.log(context);
}
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: Success
- 4: Session or line not found","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.936811-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.184794-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:44:41.305872-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.5","title":"Secret Sanitization Pipeline (P12 - Security Critical)","description":"# Feature: Secret Sanitization (SECURITY CRITICAL)

## Purpose
Prevent secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys) from being stored
in diary entries, playbook bullets, or any persistent storage.

## Risk Assessment
- **Impact**: Critical - leaked secrets could compromise systems
- **Likelihood**: High - agent sessions often contain env vars, configs
- **Priority**: P12 rated ∞ (security-critical, non-negotiable)

## High-Yield Patterns to Detect
```typescript
const SECRET_PATTERNS = [
  // AWS
  { pattern: /AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}/g, replacement: "[AWS_ACCESS_KEY]" },
  { pattern: /[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}(?=\s|$|")/g, replacement: "[AWS_SECRET_KEY]" },
  
  // Generic API keys/tokens
  { pattern: /Bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9\-\._~\+\/]+=*/g, replacement: "[BEARER_TOKEN]" },
  { pattern: /api[_-]?key["\s:=]+["']?[A-Za-z0-9\-_]{20,}["']?/gi, replacement: "[API_KEY]" },
  { pattern: /token["\s:=]+["']?[A-Za-z0-9\-_]{20,}["']?/gi, replacement: "[TOKEN]" },
  
  // Private keys
  { pattern: /-----BEGIN.*PRIVATE KEY-----[\s\S]+?-----END.*PRIVATE KEY-----/g, replacement: "[PRIVATE_KEY]" },
  
  // Passwords
  { pattern: /password["\s:=]+["'][^"']{8,}["']/gi, replacement: 'password="[REDACTED]"' },
  
  // GitHub tokens
  { pattern: /ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}/g, replacement: "[GITHUB_PAT]" },
  { pattern: /github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{22,}/g, replacement: "[GITHUB_PAT]" },
  
  // Slack tokens
  { pattern: /xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]+/g, replacement: "[SLACK_TOKEN]" },
  
  // Database URLs with credentials
  { pattern: /(postgres|mysql|mongodb):\/\/[^:]+:[^@]+@/gi, replacement: "$1://[USER]:[PASS]@" }
];
```

## Sanitization Points (WHERE to apply)
1. After cassExport(), before passing to LLM
2. Before writing DiaryEntry to disk
3. Before storing content in PlaybookBullet
4. In context output (already sanitized if stored sanitized)

## Configuration
```json
{
  "sanitization": {
    "enabled": true,
    "extraPatterns": ["INTERNAL_[A-Z_]+_KEY"]
  }
}
```

## API
```typescript
function sanitize(text: string, config: SanitizationConfig): string
```

## Testing Requirements
- Test each pattern category
- Test that sanitization is idempotent
- Test edge cases (partial matches, nested patterns)
- Verify no false positives on common code patterns","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:17:56.611313-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:17:56.611313-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.5","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:17:56.612015-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.5","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:20:11.773389-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.6","title":"Confidence Decay + Effective Score Algorithms (P1, P4)","description":"# Feature: Confidence Decay + Effective Score Algorithms

## Purpose
Implement the highest-ROI feature (20× impact): confidence decay based on
event timestamps. Old feedback matters less than recent feedback, preventing
"fossilization" where stale rules persist indefinitely.

## The Problem Being Solved
Without decay:
- A rule marked helpful 50 times 2 years ago stays "proven" forever
- Even if it's now causing problems, its historical weight dominates
- The playbook becomes fossilized with outdated rules

With decay:
- Recent feedback (last 30 days) has full weight
- Older feedback decays exponentially (half-life: 90 days)
- Rules must continue proving useful to maintain high scores

## Decay Algorithm
```typescript
function calculateDecayedValue(
  event: FeedbackEvent,
  now: Date,
  halfLifeDays: number = 90
): number {
  const eventDate = new Date(event.timestamp);
  const ageMs = now.getTime() - eventDate.getTime();
  const ageDays = ageMs / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);
  
  // Exponential decay: value = 1 * (0.5)^(age/halfLife)
  const decayFactor = Math.pow(0.5, ageDays / halfLifeDays);
  return decayFactor;
}

function getDecayedCounts(bullet: PlaybookBullet, config: Config): {
  decayedHelpful: number;
  decayedHarmful: number;
} {
  const now = new Date();
  let decayedHelpful = 0;
  let decayedHarmful = 0;
  
  for (const event of bullet.feedbackEvents) {
    const decayed = calculateDecayedValue(event, now, config.decayHalfLifeDays);
    if (event.type === "helpful") decayedHelpful += decayed;
    else decayedHarmful += decayed;
  }
  
  return { decayedHelpful, decayedHarmful };
}
```

## Effective Score Algorithm (P4 - 4× harmful multiplier)
```typescript
function getEffectiveScore(bullet: PlaybookBullet, config: Config): number {
  const { decayedHelpful, decayedHarmful } = getDecayedCounts(bullet, config);
  
  // Key insight: harmful feedback weighs 4× more than helpful
  // This surfaces problematic rules FAST
  const rawScore = decayedHelpful - (4 * decayedHarmful);
  
  // Maturity multiplier (proven rules get slight boost)
  const maturityMultiplier = {
    draft: 0.8,
    active: 1.0,
    proven: 1.2,
    retired: 0.1
  }[bullet.state] || 1.0;
  
  return rawScore * maturityMultiplier;
}
```

## Why 4× Harmful Multiplier?
- A rule that helps 3 times but hurts once is NET NEGATIVE
- This matches intuition: one bad recommendation erodes trust
- Forces rules to be consistently helpful, not just occasionally useful

## Maturity State Calculation
```typescript
function calculateMaturityState(bullet: PlaybookBullet, config: Config): State {
  const { decayedHelpful, decayedHarmful } = getDecayedCounts(bullet, config);
  const total = decayedHelpful + decayedHarmful;
  const harmfulRatio = total > 0 ? decayedHarmful / total : 0;
  
  // Transitions
  if (bullet.deprecated) return "retired";
  if (harmfulRatio > 0.3) return "retired";  // Too harmful
  if (total < 3) return "draft";              // Not enough data
  if (decayedHelpful >= 10 && harmfulRatio < 0.1) return "proven";
  return "active";
}
```

## Configuration
```json
{
  "scoring": {
    "decayHalfLifeDays": 90,
    "harmfulMultiplier": 4,
    "minFeedbackForActive": 3,
    "minHelpfulForProven": 10,
    "maxHarmfulRatioForProven": 0.1
  }
}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.7","title":"Configuration Management: Load/Save/Cascade","description":"# Feature: Configuration Management

## Purpose
Handle configuration loading, saving, and cascading from global to repo-level.

## Configuration Locations (in priority order)
1. CLI flags (highest priority)
2. `.cass/config.yaml` (repo-level)
3. `~/.cass-memory/config.json` (user-level)
4. Built-in defaults (lowest priority)

## Config Schema
```typescript
interface Config {
  // Schema version for migrations
  schema_version: number;
  
  // LLM Provider
  provider: "openai" | "anthropic" | "google";
  model: string;
  
  // Paths
  playbookPath: string;
  diaryDir: string;
  cassPath: string;
  
  // Reflection settings
  maxReflectorIterations: number;  // 1-3
  dedupSimilarityThreshold: number; // 0.0-1.0
  pruneHarmfulThreshold: number;    // Auto-prune if harmful > N
  
  // Context settings
  maxBulletsInContext: number;
  maxHistoryInContext: number;
  
  // Scoring settings
  decayHalfLifeDays: number;
  harmfulMultiplier: number;
  
  // Session settings
  sessionLookbackDays: number;
  validationLookbackDays: number;
  
  // Feature flags
  validationEnabled: boolean;
  autoReflect: boolean;
  enrichWithCrossAgent: boolean;
  semanticSearchEnabled: boolean;
  
  // Sanitization
  sanitization: {
    enabled: boolean;
    extraPatterns: string[];
  };
  
  // Logging
  verbose: boolean;
  jsonOutput: boolean;
}
```

## Functions to Implement
```typescript
// Load config with cascading priority
async function loadConfig(cliOverrides?: Partial<Config>): Promise<Config>;

// Save config (only to user-level)
async function saveConfig(config: Config): Promise<void>;

// Get default config
function getDefaultConfig(): Config;

// Merge configs (used internally)
function mergeConfigs(...configs: Partial<Config>[]): Config;

// Validate config against schema

## Purpose
Abstract LLM interactions using Vercel AI SDK, supporting multiple providers

import { createOpenAI } from "@ai-sdk/openai";
import { createAnthropic } from "@ai-sdk/anthropic";
import { createGoogleGenerativeAI } from "@ai-sdk/google";
import { generateObject, generateText } from "ai";

function getModel(config: Config) {
  const apiKey = getApiKeyFromEnv(config.provider);
  
  switch (config.provider) {
    case "openai": return createOpenAI({ apiKey })(config.model);
    case "anthropic": return createAnthropic({ apiKey })(config.model);
    case "google": return createGoogleGenerativeAI({ apiKey })(config.model);
  }
}
```

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-e8x","title":"[Playbook] loadPlaybook - Load and validate playbook from YAML","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
async function loadPlaybook(config: Config): Promise<Playbook>
```

## Purpose
Load and validate playbook from YAML file. Handles missing files, corruption, and schema validation with automatic recovery.

## Algorithm
1. Expand playbookPath from config
2. Check if file exists
3. If not exists: create empty playbook and return
4. Read file content
5. If empty: create new playbook and return
6. Parse YAML content
7. Normalize keys (snake_case → camelCase for compatibility)
8. Validate with PlaybookSchema.safeParse()
9. If invalid: backup to .backup.{timestamp} and create new
10. Return validated playbook

## Error Handling
- **ENOENT**: File doesn't exist → create empty
- **EACCES**: Permission denied → throw error
- **Parse errors**: YAML syntax errors → backup and recreate
- **Validation errors**: Schema mismatch → backup and recreate

## Backup Strategy
When corruption detected:
- Backup to: `{playbookPath}.backup.{timestamp}`
- Log warning with backup location
- Create fresh empty playbook
- Never lose user data

## Implementation Notes
- Use fs.promises for async file operations
- Use yaml.parse() for YAML parsing
- Normalize keys for backward compatibility
- Always validate against current schema version

## Dependencies
- fs/promises
- yaml library
- PlaybookSchema (Zod schema)
- createEmptyPlaybook()

## Related Functions

- savePlaybook() - writes loaded playbook back","notes":"Existing playbook helper implementations already present; no code changes made.  {"id":"cass_memory_system-efd","title":"[Types] PlaybookBullet Schema Part 1 - Identity and Scope","description":"## Overview
Define identity and scope fields for PlaybookBullet schema. These fields establish bullet uniqueness and applicability hierarchy.

## Identity Field

### id: string
- Format: 'b-{timestamp36}-{random}'
- Example: 'b-l7k9m2p4-a3f8'
- Generation: timestamp in base36 + 4-char random suffix
- Purpose: Globally unique, sortable identifier
- Immutable: Never changes after creation

## Scope Hierarchy (5 Levels)

### scope: enum
Type: 'global' | 'workspace' | 'language' | 'framework' | 'task'

Hierarchy (broadest to narrowest):

1. global - Universal rules (e.g., 'Write clear commit messages')
   - Applies: Always
   - scopeKey: Not used
   - workspace: Not used

2. workspace - Repository-specific rules
   - Applies: When in matching workspace directory
   - scopeKey: Not used (workspace field holds path)
   - workspace: '/path/to/project'
   - Example: 'Use AuthService from @/lib/auth in this project'

3. language - Language-specific rules
   - Applies: When working in files of that language
   - scopeKey: Language identifier (e.g., 'typescript', 'python', 'rust')
   - workspace: Optional (can be workspace + language)
   - Example: 'For TypeScript, prefer interfaces over types for objects'

4. framework - Framework/library-specific rules
   - Applies: When project uses that framework
   - scopeKey: Framework identifier (e.g., 'nextjs', 'react', 'django')
   - workspace: Optional
   - Example: 'For Next.js, use app router not pages router'

5. task - Task-type specific rules
   - Applies: When performing that category of work
   - scopeKey: Task category (e.g., 'authentication', 'testing', 'api-design')
   - workspace: Optional
   - Example: 'For authentication tasks, validate JWT expiration'

## Scope Filtering Logic
When retrieving bullets for context:
1. Include all 'global' scope bullets
2. If in workspace, include matching 'workspace' bullets
3. If file language detected, include matching 'language' bullets
4. If framework detected, include matching 'framework' bullets
5. If task category inferred, include matching 'task' bullets

Multiple scopes can apply simultaneously (e.g., workspace + language + task).

## Fields

### Required
- id: string - Unique identifier
- scope: 'global' | 'workspace' | 'language' | 'framework' | 'task'

### Optional
- scopeKey: string | undefined
  - Used for: language, framework, task scopes
  - Not used for: global, workspace scopes
  - Examples: 'typescript', 'react', 'testing'

- workspace: string | undefined
  - Used for: workspace scope (required)
  - Optional for: other scopes (adds workspace constraint)
  - Value: Absolute path to workspace root
  - Example: '/Users/dev/projects/my-app'

## Examples

Global Rule: { id: 'b-l7k9m2p4-a3f8', scope: 'global', content: 'Write descriptive commit messages' }

Workspace Rule: { id: 'b-m8n3p5q7-b4g9', scope: 'workspace', workspace: '/Users/dev/projects/auth-service', content: 'Use TokenValidator from @/lib/auth for all token checks' }

Language Rule: { id: 'b-n9o4q6r8-c5h0', scope: 'language', scopeKey: 'typescript', content: 'Prefer const assertions over enums for string literals' }

Framework Rule: { id: 'b-o0p5r7s9-d6i1', scope: 'framework', scopeKey: 'nextjs', workspace: '/Users/dev/projects/web-app', content: 'Use server actions in app directory, not API routes' }

Task Rule: { id: 'b-p1q6s8t0-e7j2', scope: 'task', scopeKey: 'testing', content: 'Use vitest for unit tests, playwright for e2e' }

## Acceptance Criteria
- id field with format specification
- scope enum with 5 values
- scopeKey optional field
- workspace optional field
- ID generation utility function
- Scope filtering logic implemented
- Examples for all 5 scope types
- JSDoc explaining hierarchy","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:58.006278-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:45.47468-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ehaq","title":"[CLI:stats] computeFullStats - Calculate all metrics","description":"Implements the computeFullStats function that calculates comprehensive playbook metrics.

FUNCTION SIGNATURE:
```typescript
interface PlaybookStats {
  total: number;
  byScope: { global: number; workspace: number };
  byMaturity: { 
    candidate: number; 
    established: number; 
    proven: number; 
    deprecated: number 
  };
  byType: { rule: number; antiPattern: number };
  scoreDistribution: { 
    excellent: number;    // score >= 8
    good: number;         // score 4-7
    neutral: number;      // score 0-3
    atRisk: number;       // score < 0
  };
  topPerformers: { 
    id: string; 
    score: number; 
    preview: string 
  }[];
  atRisk: { 
    id: string; 
    score: number; 
    reason: string 
  }[];
  stale: { 
    id: string; 
    daysSinceLastFeedback: number 
  }[];
  mergeCandidates: { 
    ids: string[];        // Array of 2+ similar bullet IDs
    similarity: number;   // Semantic similarity score
  }[];
}

function computeFullStats(playbook: Playbook): PlaybookStats
```

COMPUTATION LOGIC:

1. BASIC COUNTS:
   - Iterate all bullets, count by scope/maturity/type
   - Skip deprecated unless explicitly included

2. SCORE DISTRIBUTION:
   - Calculate effective score (with decay) for each bullet
   - Bucket into excellent/good/neutral/atRisk
   - Use consistent thresholds across system

3. TOP PERFORMERS:
   - Sort by effective score descending
   - Take top 10
   - Generate preview (first 60 chars of content)

4. AT RISK BULLETS:
   - Filter bullets with score < 0
   - Include reason: "Negative feedback" or "Declining effectiveness"
   - Sort by score ascending (worst first)

5. STALE BULLETS:
   - Calculate days since last helpful/harmful feedback
   - Filter bullets with >90 days (configurable threshold)
   - Include only active bullets (not deprecated)

6. MERGE CANDIDATES:
   - Compute pairwise semantic similarity (if embeddings available)
   - Find pairs/groups with similarity > 0.90
   - Exclude already-merged or deprecated bullets
   - Limit to top 5 groups

PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATIONS:
- Cache embeddings for similarity computation
- Use single pass for multiple aggregations
- Lazy compute merge candidates (expensive)
- Parallel score calculations for large playbooks

EDGE CASES:
- Empty playbook → return zeros
- No embeddings → skip merge candidates
- All bullets deprecated → show warning
- No feedback data → mark all as stale

DEPENDENCIES:
- Playbook data structure
- Score calculation utilities
- Embedding similarity (optional)
- Date/time utilities for staleness

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- All metrics accurate and consistent
- Performance <500ms for 1000 bullets

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ekh","title":"[Types] Validation Types - Scientific Validation Results","description":"## Overview
Define types for scientific validation system - results from evidence-based rule validation before adding to playbook.

## Purpose
Scientific validation prevents low-quality rules from entering the playbook by:
1. Searching historical sessions for supporting evidence
2. Analyzing success/failure patterns
3. Providing confidence scores
4. Suggesting state (active vs draft)
5. Optionally refining rule text

## ValidationResult (Simple)

Basic validation outcome for quick checks.

Fields:
- valid: boolean (pass or fail)
- reason: string (explanation of verdict)
- refinedContent: string | undefined (optional improved rule text)
- confidence: number | undefined (0-1 confidence score)

Usage: Quick validation checks, preliminary filtering

Example:
{ valid: true, reason: 'Found 5 supporting sessions with consistent pattern', confidence: 0.87 }

{ valid: false, reason: 'No evidence found in recent sessions', confidence: 0.12 }

## ValidationReport (Comprehensive)

Detailed validation analysis with evidence and recommendations.

Fields:

proposedRule: string
- The rule being validated
- Example: 'Use TokenValidator for JWT validation'

verdict: 'ACCEPT' | 'REJECT' | 'ACCEPT_WITH_CAUTION'
- ACCEPT: Strong evidence, add to playbook as active
- REJECT: Insufficient/contradictory evidence, do not add
- ACCEPT_WITH_CAUTION: Some evidence, add as draft or candidate

confidence: number
- Range: 0-1
- Based on: evidence quality, quantity, recency
- Example: 0.85 (high confidence)

evidence: Evidence[]
- Array of supporting/contradicting evidence
- Structure: { sessionPath: string, snippet: string, type: 'supporting' | 'contradicting', relevance: number }
- Example: [{ sessionPath: '/cass/2025-12-07.jsonl', snippet: 'Used TokenValidator successfully', type: 'supporting', relevance: 0.92 }]

reason: string
- Detailed explanation of verdict
- Includes: evidence summary, patterns observed, concerns
- Example: 'Found 8 supporting sessions over 30 days showing consistent use of TokenValidator for JWT validation. No contradicting patterns. Recommend adding as active rule.'

refinedRule: string | undefined
- Optional improved version of proposed rule
- Enhanced with: specificity, context, examples
- Example: 'Use TokenValidator from @/lib/auth for all JWT validation to prevent authentication bypass'

## Evidence Structure

Supporting evidence for validation.

Fields:
- sessionPath: string (path to session file)
- snippet: string (relevant excerpt from session)
- type: 'supporting' | 'contradicting'
- relevance: number (0-1, how relevant this evidence is)
- timestamp: string | undefined (when session occurred)
- agent: string | undefined (which agent)

## EvidenceGateResult

Result from pre-LLM evidence gate check.

Purpose: Fast filtering before expensive LLM validation

Fields:

passed: boolean
- True if enough evidence to proceed to LLM validation
- False if insufficient evidence (skip LLM, save cost)

reason: string
- Explanation of gate decision
- Example: 'Found 8 sessions with 6 successes, 1 failure - sufficient for validation'

suggestedState: 'draft' | 'active'
- Recommended initial state based on evidence
- draft: Low evidence (1-2 sessions)
- active: Strong evidence (5+ sessions)

sessionCount: number
- Total sessions found matching proposed rule
- Example: 8

successCount: number
- Sessions with positive outcomes
- Example: 6

failureCount: number
- Sessions with negative outcomes
- Example: 1

Example:
{ passed: true, reason: '8 sessions found (6 success, 1 failure, 1 mixed)', suggestedState: 'active', sessionCount: 8, successCount: 6, failureCount: 1 }

{ passed: false, reason: 'Only 1 session found - insufficient evidence', suggestedState: 'draft', sessionCount: 1, successCount: 1, failureCount: 0 }

## Validation Workflow

1. Evidence Gate: Run fast pre-check (EvidenceGateResult)
2. Gate Decision:
   - If passed: Proceed to LLM validation
   - If not passed: Either reject or add as draft (configurable)
3. LLM Validation: Detailed analysis (ValidationReport)
4. Apply Result:
   - ACCEPT: Add to playbook as active (or established if strong evidence)
   - ACCEPT_WITH_CAUTION: Add as draft or candidate
   - REJECT: Don't add, optionally log for future

## Configuration

validationEnabled: boolean (config, default true)
validationLookbackDays: number (config, default 90)
Evidence gate thresholds (configurable):
- minSessionsForActive: 5
- minSessionsForDraft: 2
- maxFailureRatio: 0.3 (30% failures still acceptable)

## Acceptance Criteria
- ValidationResult interface with all fields
- ValidationReport interface with comprehensive structure
- Evidence interface for evidence items
- EvidenceGateResult interface for pre-LLM gate
- Zod schemas for all types
- TypeScript exports
- Example validation reports showing ACCEPT/REJECT/CAUTION
- JSDoc documentation
- Integration with validation command","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:06.53211-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:58:26.623953-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-nf0v","title":"[Critical] V1 Minimal Command Set - Only 5 Commands","description":"# V1 Minimal Command Set - Only 5 Commands

## The Problem
Current plan has 15+ commands. This is overwhelming for new users.

## The Solution
V1 ships with only 5 commands:

### V1 Commands:
1. **context** - Get relevant context for a task
2. **mark** - Record helpful/harmful feedback
3. **playbook** - List/add/remove rules
4. **status** - Health check (doctor + stats combined)
5. **reflect** - Extract rules from sessions (optional)

### Command Details:
```bash
# 1. CONTEXT - The main command
cm context "fix the auth timeout bug"

# 2. MARK - Record feedback
cm mark rule-123 helpful
cm mark rule-456 harmful --reason "caused test failure"

# 3. PLAYBOOK - Manage rules
cm playbook             # List all rules
cm playbook add "Always check token expiry first" --category auth
cm playbook remove rule-123

# 4. STATUS - Health check
cm status               # Quick health + stats
cm status --full        # Detailed diagnostics

# 5. REFLECT - Extract rules (advanced)
cm reflect              # Process recent sessions
cm reflect --dry-run    # Preview without applying
```

### Help Text:
```bash
$ cm --help

cm v1.0.0 - Cross-Agent Memory System (cass-memory)

COMMANDS:
  context <task>    Get relevant rules and history for a task
  mark <rule> <fb>  Record helpful/harmful feedback for a rule
  playbook          List, add, or remove playbook rules
  status            Check system health and statistics
  reflect           Extract rules from recent sessions

EXAMPLES:
  cm context "fix the auth bug"
  cm mark rule-123 helpful
  cm playbook add "Always validate JWT" --category auth
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Less is more for v1
- Users can learn 5 commands
- Users cannot learn 15 commands","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:16:46.28884-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:53:59.028858-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ngd","title":"[Security] Pattern breadth testing in doctor command","description":"Implement pattern breadth testing in the doctor command to detect over-aggressive patterns.

Test purpose:
- Detect patterns that are too broad (match benign text)
- Warn about patterns with excessive false positives
- Suggest more specific alternatives

Implementation in doctor command:
```typescript
function testPatternBreadth(patterns: typeof SECRET_PATTERNS) {
  const benignSamples = [
    "The tokenizer splits text into tokens",
    "Bearer of bad news",
    "This is a password-protected file",
    "The API key concept is important"
  ];

  for (const { pattern, replacement } of patterns) {
    for (const sample of benignSamples) {
      if (pattern.test(sample)) {
        console.warn(`[doctor] Pattern too broad: ${pattern}`);
        console.warn(`  Matches benign text: "${sample}"`);
        console.warn(`  Consider more specific pattern`);
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Example warnings:
```
Warning: Pattern too broad: /token.*/gi
  Matches benign text: "The tokenizer splits text"
  Consider more specific pattern like /token["\s:=]+/
```

Tests to perform:
1. Test each SECRET_PATTERN against benign samples
2. Test user extraPatterns
3. Suggest improvements for overly broad patterns
4. Calculate false positive rate estimate

Add to doctor command output:
- "Sanitization Pattern Health"
- List any overly broad patterns
- Suggest fixes

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-pwp","title":"[Generator] formatLastHelpful - Human-readable feedback time","description":"Format bullet feedback timestamps as human-readable relative time.

SIGNATURE:
function formatLastHelpful(bullet: PlaybookBullet): string

PURPOSE:
Convert the most recent helpful event timestamp into a user-friendly relative time string for display in context output. Helps users understand recency of validation.

INPUT:
PlaybookBullet with events: Array<{
  type: 'helpful' | 'unhelpful',
  timestamp: Date,
  sessionPath?: string,
  notes?: string
}>

LOGIC:
1. Find most recent event where type === 'helpful'
2. If no helpful events exist, return 'Never'
3. Calculate time difference from now
4. Format based on magnitude:
   - < 1 hour: '{minutes} minutes ago'
   - < 24 hours: '{hours} hours ago'
   - < 7 days: '{days} days ago'
   - < 30 days: '{weeks} weeks ago'
   - < 365 days: '{months} months ago'
   - >= 365 days: '{years} years ago'

EXAMPLES:
- Last helpful 45 minutes ago → '45 minutes ago'
- Last helpful 3 hours ago → '3 hours ago'
- Last helpful 2 days ago → '2 days ago'
- Last helpful 3 weeks ago → '3 weeks ago'
- Last helpful 5 months ago → '5 months ago'
- Last helpful 2 years ago → '2 years ago'
- No helpful events → 'Never'

ROUNDING:
- Always round down (floor)
- 1.9 days → '1 day ago' (not 2)
- This is conventional for relative time

SPECIAL CASES:
- 'just now' for < 1 minute (optional)
- 'today' for same calendar day (optional)
- 'yesterday' for previous calendar day (optional)

FORMATTING OPTIONS:
Basic: '{n} {unit}s ago'
Friendly: 'last week', 'yesterday', 'just now'

IMPLEMENTATION:
Use date-fns or similar library for robust relative time:
import { formatDistanceToNow } from 'date-fns';
return formatDistanceToNow(lastHelpfulEvent.timestamp, { addSuffix: true });

OUTPUT USAGE:
Appears in context markdown:
  - Score: 4.2 | Last helpful: 2 days ago

Helps user assess:
- Recency of validation (recent = high confidence)
- Staleness (never helpful = low confidence)
- Temporal patterns (helpful 2 years ago = may be outdated)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:02.833061-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:28.707149-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-qbdq","title":"[Critical] Zero-Config Quick Start Mode - Immediate Value Without Setup","description":"# Zero-Config Quick Start Mode

## The Problem
Current plan requires: init → config → reflect → wait for sessions → curate → then finally get value.
This is too many steps before users see ANY benefit. Most will abandon before step 3.

## The Solution
Make `cm context "my task"` work IMMEDIATELY, even with zero setup:

### Day 1 (No Setup Required):
```bash
# User just runs this - no init, no config
cm context "fix the auth bug"

# Output (even with empty playbook):
# Checking your recent agent sessions...
# Found 3 relevant sessions from Claude Code and Cursor
# 
# ## Relevant Past Work:
# - Session from 2 hours ago: Fixed JWT validation (similar: auth)
